questions.question: Which of the following is correct based on Molecular orbital theory for peroxide ion?

options.A: Its bond order is two and it is diamagnetic
options.B: Its bond order is one and it is paramagnetic
options.C: Its bond order is two and it is paramagnetic
options.D: Its bond order is one and it is diamagnetic

questions.explanation: The peroxide ion ($O_2^{2-}$) has a total of 18 electrons, which fills the molecular orbitals up to the $\pi^* 2p_x$ and $\pi^* 2p_y$ levels. The bond order is calculated as $\frac{1}{2}(10 - 8) = 1$ using the number of bonding and antibonding electrons. Since all electrons are paired in these orbitals, the ion is diamagnetic.
